commands.name: random_placement
commands.description: Places player starting positions randomly. Mutually exclusive with grouped_by_team and direct_placement.
commands.notes: Sourced from the Definitive RMS Scripting Guide (Zetnus).

commands.name: grouped_by_team
commands.description: Places player starting positions grouped by team.

commands.name: direct_placement
commands.description: Places players directly, in a fixed arrangement rather than randomized.

commands.name: nomad_resources
commands.description: Grants players a nomad-style starting resource/unit set instead of a fixed base.

commands.name: force_nomad_treaty
commands.description: Forces nomad-treaty rules for this map. DE only.

commands.name: behavior_version
commands.description: Selects generation-behavior version for backward compatibility. DE only.

commands.name: override_map_size
commands.description: Overrides the lobby-selected map size with an explicit tile dimension.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: Values outside this range are clamped.

commands.name: set_gaia_civilization
commands.description: Sets the civilization used for Gaia (neutral) units/buildings on this map. DE only.

commands.name: ai_info_map_type
commands.description: Tells the AI what kind of map this is, affecting its strategy choices.
commands.notes: Exact behavior differs slightly per game version per the source guide.

commands.name: effect_amount
commands.description: Applies a flat-amount gameplay effect (resource/attribute/tech modification) at map start.

arguments.name: amount
arguments.description: DE treats this as a float.

commands.name: effect_percent
commands.description: Same shape as effect_amount, but the value is treated as a percentage (divided by 100).

commands.name: guard_state
commands.description: Sets a guard-resource state; only one guard_state is allowed per script.

arguments.name: resourceDelta
arguments.description: DE treats this as a float.

commands.name: terrain_state
commands.description: Legacy terrain-state configuration.
commands.notes: Marked UP-only (pre-DE) in the source guide; behavior in DE unconfirmed.

commands.name: weather_type
commands.description: Sets visual weather/lighting parameters.
commands.notes: Marked UP-only (pre-DE) in the source guide; DE support unconfirmed.

commands.name: water_definition
commands.description: Selects a named water-type definition (must be #const-defined). DE only.

commands.name: base_terrain
commands.description: Sets the default terrain the whole map starts as, before any lands/terrains are placed.
commands.notes: The same word "base_terrain" is also a valid attribute inside create_elevation/create_terrain blocks — see the attributes[] entry.

commands.name: base_layer
commands.description: Sets a secondary base terrain layer. DE only.

commands.name: enable_waves
commands.description: Toggles shoreline wave visuals. DE only.

commands.name: create_player_lands
commands.description: Creates one land per player, distributing them per the block's attributes. Takes the same attribute set as create_land.

commands.name: create_land
commands.description: Creates one land mass (typically non-player territory — islands, center land, etc). Takes the same attribute set as create_player_lands.

commands.name: create_elevation
commands.description: Raises or lowers terrain elevation in clumps.

commands.name: cliff_type
commands.description: Sets the visual cliff material for this map. DE only.

arguments.name: type
arguments.description: One of CT_GRANITE, CT_DESERT, CT_SNOW, CT_MARBLE, CT_LIMESTONE.

commands.name: min_number_of_cliffs
commands.description: Minimum number of cliff formations to generate.

commands.name: max_number_of_cliffs
commands.description: Maximum number of cliff formations to generate.

commands.name: min_length_of_cliff
commands.description: Minimum length of each cliff formation.

commands.name: max_length_of_cliff
commands.description: Maximum length of each cliff formation.

commands.name: cliff_curliness
commands.description: How often a cliff changes direction along its length — low values for straight cliffs, high for winding ones.
commands.notes: Range/default confirmed; the source guide's fuller description was cut off mid-sentence in the fetch used to populate this file.

commands.name: min_distance_cliffs
commands.description: Minimum distance between separate cliff formations.
commands.notes: Name confirmed via the guide's syntax skeleton only — argument shape and default not reached before the source fetch was cut off. Re-verify before relying on the argument type.

commands.name: min_terrain_distance
commands.description: Minimum distance a cliff must keep from certain terrain.
commands.notes: Name confirmed via the guide's syntax skeleton only — same caveat as min_distance_cliffs.

commands.name: color_correction
commands.description: Applies a color-correction preset to terrain rendering.
commands.notes: Name only, from the guide's syntax skeleton — full documentation for TERRAIN_GENERATION wasn't reached before the source fetch was cut off. Verify before trusting the argument shape.

commands.name: create_terrain
commands.description: Paints patches of a terrain type onto the map.
commands.notes: Command itself and the attributes base_terrain/land_percent/number_of_clumps/height_limits are confirmed via a real working example in the source guide: create_terrain DESERT { base_terrain GRASS  land_percent 100  number_of_clumps 9320  height_limits 1 7 }. The rest of this attribute list is name-only (see the attributes[] entries).

commands.name: accumulate_connections
commands.description: Enables connection accumulation mode for subsequent connection commands.
commands.notes: Name only, from the guide's syntax skeleton — full CONNECTION_GENERATION documentation wasn't reached before the source fetch was cut off.

commands.name: create_connect_all_players_land
commands.description: Connects all players' lands to each other.
commands.notes: Name only, confirmed spelled with singular "land" (not "lands") in the guide's syntax skeleton — correcting an earlier unverified assumption used in src/editor/aoe2RmsLanguage.ts that had the plural. Recheck the aoe2RmsLanguage.ts COMMANDS list against this file.

commands.name: create_connect_teams_lands
commands.description: Connects each team's lands to each other, but not to other teams.
commands.notes: Name only, from the guide's syntax skeleton.

commands.name: create_connect_all_lands
commands.description: Connects every land on the map to every other land, player or not.
commands.notes: Name only, from the guide's syntax skeleton.

commands.name: create_connect_same_land_zones
commands.description: Connects lands that share the same zone.
commands.notes: Name only, from the guide's syntax skeleton.

commands.name: create_connect_land_zones
commands.description: Connects two specific zones to each other.
commands.notes: Name only, from the guide's syntax skeleton.

commands.name: create_connect_to_nonplayer_land
commands.description: Connects player lands to a specific non-player land.
commands.notes: Name only, from the guide's syntax skeleton.

commands.name: create_actor_area
commands.description: Defines a named area (referenced by ID) used to constrain later object placement via actor_area/actor_area_to_place_in.
commands.notes: Name and 4-integer argument count confirmed via the guide's syntax skeleton; exact meaning of each argument not reached before the source fetch was cut off.

commands.name: create_object_group
commands.description: Places a themed group of objects (e.g. a mixed forage/relic cluster) as a unit.
commands.notes: Name only, from the guide's syntax skeleton.

commands.name: create_object
commands.description: Places objects (resources, units, buildings) on the map, either bare (create_object GOLD) or with a block of placement-constraining attributes.
commands.notes: Command confirmed via two real working examples in the source guide: bare `create_object MONUMENT` and blocked `create_object GOLD { place_on_specific_land_id 13 }`. Most of the attribute list beyond place_on_specific_land_id is name-only — see individual attributes[] entries.

attributes.name: terrain_type
attributes.description: The terrain this land is made of.

attributes.name: land_percent
attributes.description: Share of the map this land should occupy.

attributes.name: number_of_tiles
attributes.description: Absolute tile count for this land, instead of a percentage.

attributes.name: base_size
attributes.description: Base radius of the land before growth/fuzziness is applied.

attributes.name: set_circular_base
attributes.description: Forces the land's base shape to be circular. DE only.

attributes.name: generate_mode
attributes.description: Legacy generation-mode toggle. DE only.

attributes.name: land_position
attributes.description: Fixes the land's position as a percentage of map dimensions. Ignores border settings.

attributes.name: circle_radius
attributes.description: Radius (as a percent of map size) for a circular land, plus variance. DE only.

attributes.name: left_border

arguments.name: percent
arguments.cautionMessage: Negative border values are valid RMS, but can crash the game if they push the land origin outside the map — pair a negative border with an explicit land_position to keep the origin on-map.

attributes.description: Minimum distance from the map's left edge.
attributes.notes: Guide documents 0-99 as the typical range but explicitly allows negative values, as long as the land origin stays inside the map (e.g. via land_position or a large enough base_size) — so no lower bound is enforced here. Float values accepted since Update 153015.

attributes.name: right_border

arguments.name: percent
arguments.cautionMessage: Negative border values are valid RMS, but can crash the game if they push the land origin outside the map — pair a negative border with an explicit land_position to keep the origin on-map.

attributes.description: Minimum distance from the map's right edge.
attributes.notes: Guide documents 0-99 as the typical range but explicitly allows negative values, as long as the land origin stays inside the map (e.g. via land_position or a large enough base_size) — so no lower bound is enforced here. Float values accepted since Update 153015.

attributes.name: top_border

arguments.name: percent
arguments.cautionMessage: Negative border values are valid RMS, but can crash the game if they push the land origin outside the map — pair a negative border with an explicit land_position to keep the origin on-map.

attributes.description: Minimum distance from the map's top edge.
attributes.notes: Guide documents 0-99 as the typical range but explicitly allows negative values, as long as the land origin stays inside the map (e.g. via land_position or a large enough base_size) — so no lower bound is enforced here. Float values accepted since Update 153015. BUG (guide-documented): asymmetric borders for player lands can misbehave when top_border is larger than the other borders — always pair it with another border.

attributes.name: bottom_border

arguments.name: percent
arguments.cautionMessage: Negative border values are valid RMS, but can crash the game if they push the land origin outside the map — pair a negative border with an explicit land_position to keep the origin on-map.

attributes.description: Minimum distance from the map's bottom edge.
attributes.notes: Guide documents 0-99 as the typical range but explicitly allows negative values, as long as the land origin stays inside the map (e.g. via land_position or a large enough base_size) — so no lower bound is enforced here. Float values accepted since Update 153015.

attributes.name: border_fuzziness
attributes.description: Randomizes land edges instead of a hard border.

attributes.name: clumping_factor

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Useful range roughly 0-40; negative values produce snakey shapes.

attributes.description: Controls how blobby vs. scattered a land/terrain's shape is.

attributes.name: land_conformity
attributes.description: DE-only land-shape parameter. Noted as buggy in the source guide.
attributes.notes: Guide explicitly flags this as buggy — worth a real playtest before relying on it.

attributes.name: base_elevation
attributes.description: Elevation level this land starts at. Requires an <ELEVATION_GENERATION> section to be present.

attributes.name: assign_to_player
attributes.description: Assigns this land to a specific player number.

attributes.name: assign_to
attributes.description: Assigns this land by player, color, or team.

attributes.name: zone
attributes.description: Assigns this land to a numbered zone, used later for terrain/object placement scoping.

attributes.name: set_zone_by_team
attributes.description: Assigns this land's zone automatically by team.

attributes.name: set_zone_randomly
attributes.description: Assigns this land's zone randomly.

attributes.name: other_zone_avoidance_distance
attributes.description: Minimum distance to keep from lands in other zones.

attributes.name: min_placement_distance
attributes.description: Minimum distance between instances of this land. Defaults to other_zone_avoidance_distance's value.

attributes.name: land_id
attributes.description: Names this land so later commands (e.g. place_on_specific_land_id) can target it.

attributes.name: number_of_clumps
attributes.description: How many separate blobs to split the placement into.

attributes.name: set_scale_by_size
attributes.description: Scales placement amount by map size.

attributes.name: set_scale_by_groups
attributes.description: Scales placement amount by number of clumps/groups instead of map size.

attributes.name: spacing
attributes.description: Minimum spacing between elevation clumps.

attributes.name: enable_balanced_elevation
attributes.description: Tries to keep elevation generation fair across player starting areas. DE only.

attributes.name: base_terrain
attributes.description: Sets the default/underlying terrain for this block (elevation clump or terrain patch), before the block's own terrain is layered on.
attributes.notes: Same word as the standalone LAND_GENERATION command of the same name (see commands[]) — different context, both real; RMS reuses identifiers between command and attribute position.

attributes.name: base_layer
attributes.description: Sets a secondary base terrain layer for this block. DE only.
attributes.notes: Same word as the standalone LAND_GENERATION command of the same name (see commands[]).

attributes.name: beach_terrain
attributes.description: Terrain used for the shoreline transition next to this terrain patch.

attributes.name: terrain_mask
attributes.description: Restricts which underlying terrains this patch can be painted over.

attributes.name: spacing_to_other_terrain_types
attributes.description: Minimum spacing to keep from other terrain-generation patches.

attributes.name: spacing_to_specific_terrain
attributes.description: Minimum spacing to keep from a specific named terrain.

attributes.name: set_flat_terrain_only
attributes.description: Only paints this terrain onto flat (non-elevated) ground.

attributes.name: set_avoid_player_start_areas
attributes.description: Keeps this terrain away from player starting areas.

attributes.name: height_limits
attributes.description: Restricts this terrain to a range of elevation levels.
attributes.notes: Confirmed via the working create_terrain example in the source guide.

attributes.name: default_terrain_replacement
attributes.description: Terrain used to pave connections by default.
attributes.notes: Name only — full CONNECTION_GENERATION docs weren't reached in the source fetch.

attributes.name: replace_terrain
attributes.description: Replaces one terrain with another along the connection path.

attributes.name: terrain_cost
attributes.description: Pathfinding cost weight for a terrain when routing this connection.

attributes.name: terrain_size
attributes.description: Width/spacing of a terrain band used along the connection path.

attributes.name: add_object
attributes.description: Adds an object type to an object group, weighted by percent chance.
attributes.notes: Name only — full OBJECTS_GENERATION docs weren't reached in the source fetch.

attributes.name: number_of_objects
attributes.description: How many of this object to place.

attributes.name: number_of_groups
attributes.description: How many separate clusters to split the placement into.

attributes.name: group_variance
attributes.description: Randomizes how many objects land in each group.

attributes.name: group_placement_radius
attributes.description: Keeps grouped objects within this many tiles of the group's center.

attributes.name: set_tight_grouping
attributes.description: Places grouped objects on adjacent tiles.

attributes.name: set_loose_grouping
attributes.description: Allows more spread between grouped objects.

attributes.name: min_connected_tiles
attributes.description: Minimum contiguous placeable area required.

attributes.name: resource_delta
attributes.description: Adjusts the resource amount carried by placed objects.

attributes.name: second_object
attributes.description: A secondary object type placed alongside the primary one.

attributes.name: set_scaling_to_map_size
attributes.description: Scales object count by map size.

attributes.name: set_scaling_to_player_number
attributes.description: Scales object count by number of players.

attributes.name: set_place_for_every_player
attributes.description: Ensures every player's land gets a copy of this placement.
attributes.notes: Behavior confirmed by the official Age of Empires support summary referencing this attribute; exact argument shape not independently verified here.

attributes.name: place_on_specific_land_id
attributes.description: Restricts placement to a land tagged with land_id.
attributes.notes: Confirmed via the working create_object example in the source guide: create_object GOLD { place_on_specific_land_id 13 }.

attributes.name: avoid_other_land_zones
attributes.description: Keeps placement away from other zones' lands.

attributes.name: generate_for_first_land_only
attributes.description: Restricts generation to only the first land created.

attributes.name: set_gaia_object_only
attributes.description: Placed objects belong to Gaia (neutral) rather than a player.

attributes.name: set_gaia_unconvertible
attributes.description: Prevents Gaia-owned placed objects from being converted.

attributes.name: set_building_capturable
attributes.description: Allows placed Gaia buildings to be captured.

attributes.name: make_indestructible
attributes.description: Placed objects cannot be destroyed.

attributes.name: min_distance_to_players
attributes.description: Minimum distance to keep from any player's starting area.

attributes.name: max_distance_to_players
attributes.description: Maximum distance to allow from any player's starting area.

attributes.name: set_circular_placement
attributes.description: Constrains placement to a circular area.

attributes.name: terrain_to_place_on
attributes.description: Restricts placement to a specific terrain type.
attributes.notes: Widely used and unambiguous in community scripts; the argument shape (single terrain constant) is well established even though this exact source fetch didn't reach a formal description.

attributes.name: layer_to_place_on
attributes.description: Restricts placement to a specific terrain layer.

attributes.name: ignore_terrain_restrictions
attributes.description: Bypasses normal terrain-placement rules for this object.

attributes.name: max_distance_to_other_zones
attributes.description: Maximum distance allowed from other zones.

attributes.name: place_on_forest_zone
attributes.description: Places objects only on, and directly next to, tiles with trees on them (including straggler trees and scenario editor trees).
attributes.notes: Boolean flag — takes NO argument, unlike avoid_forest_zone (Distance). Confirmed against the archived guide's own worked examples (`place_on_forest_zone` used bare). Was previously wrongly given an integer `distance` argument, which drew a false RMS0201 warning on every real use.

attributes.name: avoid_forest_zone
attributes.description: Keeps placement away from forest zones.

attributes.name: avoid_cliff_zone
attributes.description: Keeps placement away from cliff zones.

attributes.name: min_distance_to_map_edge
attributes.description: Minimum distance to keep from the edge of the map.

attributes.name: min_distance_group_placement
attributes.description: Minimum distance between separate placement groups.
attributes.notes: Widely used and unambiguous in community scripts, though this exact source fetch only gave the name — treat the argument shape as high-confidence but not source-verified.

attributes.name: temp_min_distance_group_placement
attributes.description: Temporary override of min_distance_group_placement for this placement only.

attributes.name: find_closest
attributes.description: Prefers the closest valid location rather than a random valid one.

attributes.name: find_closest_to_map_center
attributes.description: Prefers locations closest to the map's center.

attributes.name: find_closest_to_map_edge
attributes.description: Prefers locations closest to the map's edge.

attributes.name: enable_tile_shuffling
attributes.description: Randomizes tile evaluation order during placement search.

attributes.name: require_path
attributes.description: Requires a valid path of the given type to reach this placement.

attributes.name: force_placement
attributes.description: Forces placement even if normal constraints would reject it.

attributes.name: actor_area
attributes.description: Associates this placement with an actor area created by create_actor_area.

attributes.name: actor_area_radius
attributes.description: Radius of the associated actor area.

attributes.name: override_actor_radius_if_required
attributes.description: Allows the actor area radius to be overridden if placement would otherwise fail.

attributes.name: actor_area_to_place_in
attributes.description: Restricts placement to inside a specific actor area.
attributes.notes: Widely used and unambiguous in community scripts, though this exact source fetch only gave the name.

attributes.name: avoid_actor_area
attributes.description: Keeps placement away from a specific actor area.

attributes.name: avoid_all_actor_areas
attributes.description: Keeps placement away from every defined actor area.

attributes.name: set_facet
attributes.description: Sets the visual facet/rotation variant used for placed objects.

attributes.name: match_player_civ
attributes.description: Placed objects visually match the owning player's civilization.

directives.name: #const
directives.description: Defines a named integer constant usable anywhere a number is expected.
directives.notes: Confirmed via real examples in the source guide, e.g. #const OUTLAW 158.

directives.name: #define
directives.description: Defines a preprocessor flag (no value) for use with if/elseif conditionals.

directives.name: #include_drs
directives.description: Includes a script fragment from a DRS-packed resource file.

directives.name: #includeXS
directives.description: Includes an XS script file. DE feature.

directives.name: #undefine
directives.notes: Used in this project's own test-maps/sample.rms by convention, but not directly confirmed in the source guide fetch used to populate this file (that fetch didn't reach the guide's Global Syntax section) — real-world community usage strongly suggests it exists, but re-verify before shipping.

directives.name: #include
directives.description: Includes a plain script fragment file.
directives.notes: Only #include_drs and #includeXS were confirmed in the source fetch — plain #include is a common assumption but wasn't independently confirmed. Verify before relying on it.

directives.name: #ifdef
directives.description: Preprocessor conditional: includes the following block only if the named flag is #define'd.
directives.notes: Not reached in the source fetch (Global Syntax section wasn't in the fetched portion) — commonly documented elsewhere in the community, but re-verify.

directives.name: #ifndef
directives.description: Preprocessor conditional: includes the following block only if the named flag is NOT #define'd.

directives.name: #else
directives.description: Preprocessor-level else, distinct from the runtime else control keyword. Pairs with #ifdef/#ifndef.

directives.name: #endif
directives.description: Closes a preprocessor #ifdef/#ifndef block. Distinct from the runtime endif control keyword.

controlKeywords.name: if
controlKeywords.description: Begins a runtime conditional block, evaluated during generation (not at parse time — contrast with #ifdef).

controlKeywords.name: elseif
controlKeywords.description: Additional branch of a runtime if/elseif/else/endif chain.

controlKeywords.name: else
controlKeywords.description: Fallback branch of a runtime if/elseif/else/endif chain.

controlKeywords.name: start_random
controlKeywords.description: Begins a block of percent_chance-weighted alternatives, closed by end_random.

controlKeywords.name: percent_chance
controlKeywords.description: Inside a start_random block, gives one alternative's weight (should sum to 100 across the block).
